trend: Mild bearish for the next 1-3 trading days while NIFTY stays below 24250-24300; recovery above 24342 would neutralize the bearish read, while loss of 24077/24000 confirms downside.
entry: Direction: sell rallies near 24250-24300 or sell breakdown below 24142; expected probability about 56%; risk-reward about 1:1.5 to target1 and 1:2.3 to target2.
stoploss: Above 24342 on sustained intraday acceptance or above 24350 on closing basis
bullish_reasons: NIFTY recovered from a 24000.2 intraday low and was near flat around 24204 at the 14:10 IST NiftyTrader snapshot.
FII cash flow was positive at +2603.7 Cr and DII cash flow was positive at +2019.7 Cr as of 2026-07-10.
NIFTY is slightly above max pain 24200, which prevents immediate breakdown confirmation.
Change-OI PCR is strong at 2.7950 and standing PCR is high at 1.6603, indicating put-side activity and some support defence.
IT sector strength is cushioning the index, with TCS, Infosys and HCL Technologies among the top positive contributors.
bearish_reasons: Market bias bucket deteriorated from the previous STRONG BULLISH read because score fell from +8 to -3.
India VIX rose to about 13.35-13.37, up roughly 1.10-1.12 points, reducing confidence in buy-on-dips setups.
PCR at 1.6603 is above the 1.4 overbought threshold in the scoring model, so it is scored bearish despite put writing.
Highest Call OI shifted down to 24200, creating immediate overhead supply; highest Put OI shifted down to 24000.
FII index futures remain net short around -254711 contracts, despite positive cash buying.
Brent crude jumped around 4% toward $79 amid renewed Middle East tension, a macro negative for India.
USDINR opened sharply weaker near 95.7050 versus 95.3250, reflecting rupee pressure.
Asian cues are weak overall: Shanghai -2.06% and Nikkei -1.92%, with Hang Seng only slightly positive.
Nifty 50 breadth is weak at 21 advances versus 29 declines, A/D ratio 0.72.
Major news flow is negative: oil shock, rupee weakness, geopolitical escalation and profit-booking after a two-session rally.
Exact public VWAP and full CPR bands are unavailable from accessible text sources; pivot levels were used instead.
alerts: OI Shift: highest Call OI rolled down from 24500 to 24200 and highest Put OI rolled down from 24200 to 24000.
PCR Changes: NIFTY PCR moved to 1.6603, above the model's 1.4 bearish/overheated threshold.
VIX Spike: India VIX rose to about 13.35-13.37, up around 1.10-1.12 points.
Gap Down Recovery: NIFTY opened near 24039 after previous close 24206.9 and recovered near flat, so breakdown needs confirmation below 24077/24000.
Strong Put Writing: change-OI PCR is 2.7950, but the high standing PCR makes this a conflicted rather than clean bullish signal.
meaningful_changes: No material change versus the 14:31 IST cycle: bias remains MILD BEARISH, score remains -3 and confidence remains 56%.
